reviews.review_description: I almost didn't buy this game after seeing the photos. I thought the graphics looked boring. Just trust me & try it.. its fun & easy to play. It's definitely a workout, dodging bullets. But you can also adjust many settings to make it easier, i.e. they don't shoot at you so theres nothing to dodge. Even my 6-year-old niece enjoys it. The user-created songs are awesome, too. I didn't realize they were there for months & now those are almost all I play. This is my 2nd most played game & after 4 months, I still play almost daily. No complaints.

While this isn’t technically a Fitness app, you get an amazing workout whether you want to or not!  I wouldn’t recommend it to people new to VR. The first time I tried to play it. I had no idea what I was doing and just got killed immediately but once I was a more seasoned VR gamer, I was able to play it and immediately loved it ￼￼

When I was thinking to buy this game, I was looking through its photos and thought ‘The graphics are bad, so probably the music quality too, maybe it’s not worth buying’. But I reconsidered it and decided to buy it, and after playing through both the 2089 campaign and Smoke & Thunder campaign, I was shocked to find that the game on the inside is great. I really recommend to first play ‘The Grave’ and ‘Black Magic’ firstly to unlock the campaigns, and once you do, play the 2089 campaign firstly, it will train you to be ready for the Smoke & Thunder campaign since it’s harder.

Really recommend this game, my personal rating: 10/10

This is the game that I like to pick up for a quick five minutes of action and fun.
I appreciate everything the developers have done, and the skill progression I have seen for myself as I have practiced has been so satisfying!
It's a bloody fun game. Definitely want to buy this game.

reviews.review_title: it won't let me play a game
reviews.review_description: i got this with meta horizon + but it just wouldn't let me play
This game is so much fun to play alone against someone make a little room and just go crazy!
What a great game...and a good workout too! When is Pistol Whip 2 coming out?!
I love how customizable it is. And actually a decent workout.

I love shooting games. My eldest came to vist a few years back with meta VR and told dad, "me" to try it. This year during super black Friday events I bought two head sets. Extra batteries, cooling fans everything 😆.  All I know is this breaks a sweat! I live in the woods. Cut down my own trees for firewood and so forth. But man this is fun. I remember dial up phones. Way before the internet. Yes, im a dying bread but its only been 40+ years and I can only imagine what 5 years from now will be like. I love this game. Winter I can still work like im outside. Fun! Especially if you like shooting 🔫

I am a 57 year old looking for cardio that won’t kill me.  This game is so much fun! Please, please add on more scenes for purchase.  The music and scenes are amazing.  I play them all at the hardest level and need more!!!!

I am a 38 yr old out shape Mom. I started playing this and got other work out apps, but the is by far the BEST! I can’t get motivated to squat or work out, but when your dodging bullets to music and accuracy prevents you from dodging more bullets count me in! Amazing settings, I started with some accessibility modifiers on like extra amour, but now I do dev recommendations and my legs feel like rubber bands but I can’t stop playing. I highly recommend to anyone wanting a workout or easing into it. Great graphics not dizzy or nauseous! Great game!

reviews.review_title: The game I always return to
reviews.review_description: In my original review I said that I doubt the longevity. There were only a few tracks, the same enemies always, and the music wasn't exactly to my taste. I'm glad to say that's no longer the case. I don't play a lot these days, but when I put on my Quest 2, Pistol Whip is what I play. It's easy enough that it can be played casually. The newer levels aren't trivial even at easy, but I can finish them, the older ones can be played at easy to relax or normal for a bit more challenge. There is enough variety that I can start a few levels and feel like a bada55 (really, this doesn't pass the profanity filter?) shooting to music while dodging bullets flying in slow motion. The core gameplay is fun, and it provides a moderate workout.

Pistol Whip, as you can see from the trailer, is a retro-style on-rails shooter with modern graphics and, of course, the upgrade from arcade machine to VR.  You are moved forward at a steady pace while enemies pop out and you get points for shooting them (extra for shooting to the beat and for headshots).  More importantly, downing them will reduce the avalanche of bullets coming at you, which can pass through your body but you must dodge around with your head. You normally have armor, which can protect you from a single hit and be regenerated by hitting a certain number of shots (determined by difficulty settings) or a single melee hit.  There are also obstacles in the path that you will need to lean your head around, sometimes weaving back and forth quite quickly, though these can be turned off in the settings.  You reload by flipping the gun up or down (I highly suggest up, which is an option in settings, as this is much more comfortable and also allows you to reload while mimicking recoil), and there is HEAVY auto-aim, so you will almost never miss.  Most stages are about 5 minutes, I think.  Difficulty options are a good spread.

There are quite a few game modes, including single songs, sets of songs (that must be completed without dying), daily and weekly challenges, and, best of all, 2 campaigns - a sci-fi theme and a Wild West theme.  Each campaign is a series of about 5 single songs, with some light story cutscenes in between, and each features a special gun augmentation after the first song, which substantially changes the gun’s effect.  For the sci-fi, you get a machine pistol, with 4-shot rapid-fire and a large clip.  For the west, you get an explosive, double-damage AoE shot, with a small clip.  Both feel great.  Each campaign ends with a big boss fight.  While both are cool, I really loved the Wild West style and story.

It can really be a rhythm game or not, depending on whether you choose to care about that aspect.  You get more points for shooting to the beat but you can also just choose to ignore that.  I don’t personally care about scores but I do find it’s a little more engaging when you keep a tempo to your shots.

There are also a good number of mods you can apply yourself or which appear in challenges, like dual-wielding, no armor, no auto-aim (humbling!), etc.  There are also some different guns, with various clip size, sound effects, and many cosmetic options.

So, there’s plenty to do!  It’s challenging, there’s endless content, it’s a bit of a workout, it makes you feel like an action hero, and the campaigns are great.  I highly recommend it!
Love this game. Add more campaigns!

What can I say about this game that hasn’t been said already. I don’t typically like rhythm games, but this is just a masterpiece!

The game plays wonderfully, it’s so fun to shoot to the beat of the music, and dodge bullets like the Matrix! The visuals are stunning despite it not trying to look like a hyper realistic game. Every weapon type is fun to use, the fact that you can customize the weapons is above and beyond! The campaigns are really fun and really engaging, and the other levels are really fun. The soundtrack for this game hits hard, the songs are just so good!

There’s nothing more I can say that someone else probably hasn’t said before. This is the best game on VR, and you’d be a fool to not play it!

Easily one of the best games in VR - absolutely fantastic fun, great workout and amazing experience. The only slight downside is that the song selection is limited and a lot of the remixes can't actually be launched (I assume because they use copyrighted music), so you end up repeating them pretty quickly. It's also missing a few obvious reference points - I am dying for some great James Bond style levels but there aren't any. In general though, highly recommended and a must buy for any VR user.

reviews.review_title: Enough of beat sabers ? Get this one !
reviews.review_description: Mill's Body Combat was my first purchased game. Loved it. I even plan to come back to it soon. Beat Sabers was as good as they say. But THIS ! Why did it took me so long before getting it ! I find it more engaging than Beat Saber and it brought me back to wanting to boxe again. Better way of explain this game : a mix between beat saber and john wick 😅

I’ve tried so many games to keep me engaged and help with cardio… this 100% hit the mark and works great. It doesn’t pretend to be a super high-graphic FPS. It stays perfectly in its lane of lower poly graphics and keeps you moving without making you sick. I get easily nauseated by some games but this is just so well made that I played until my headset died! I can’t wait to come back tomorrow for my daily workout and do it again.

Junk. If you want a non-shooter shooter get Vendetta instead. I don’t recommend that one either.

Tons of really good gun games out there…
Seriously fun game
Must try game. Awesome John wick style game play.

This is such a fun game!! Especially when you hear those shots/reloads on beat, you feel like John Wick. I'd love to see an animated short or physical graphic novel of the campaign stories. 2089 was so cool and I wanted to see more of Asimov's story!! Pretty cool you went with that name 😎. 

Only thing I don't like.. I waited so long to jump into this!!

So much fun, the storys are brilliant, great workout. Think I managed about 100 jumping squats playing this game. Only stopped as the wife raised concerns I was getting closer to the wall. 
Only downside is people are likely to film you while you're flying through the levels.

I lost 4 pounds in a week bye playing this every day it's very good graphics must buy it's more for experienced players and I've been playing for 3 years must buy GREAT GAME also helped me sweat very fun game

reviews.review_title: A sedate mode would be nice
reviews.review_description: The game is great, but it would be nice to have some scenes that are just basic. Maybe a road through a mining town in the wild west, with standard wooden buildings. No flashy colours and dizzying movement and wild music. Or even maybe travelling through a wood with trees etc. but again less flashy colours and graphics.

reviews.review_title: An arcade shooter with tons of content
reviews.review_description: This game takes me back to the days of Time Crisis in the arcade but it’s even more immersive. I get a good workout while having fun. I do find it a bit confusing on how to get to the other levels and campaigns though.

This is one of those games where you want to buy the developers a drink, give them a hi-five and a hug, and thank them for making this game part of your life. Well done! The only improvement I could think of is clearer, more organized and more descriptive menus. I YouTubed some instructions for the menus so I could get around quicker.

This game has me feeling like I'm John Wick! I only had the game for 2 days and my legs are so sore.Def a workout I was drenched in sweat but the game is so fun that I'm not going to stop playing.  Sat in my wishlist for months I'm so glad I purchased it.

reviews.review_title: One of the best games ever made
reviews.review_description: This will get you fit if you play it pretty often. I love the music that syncs to the movement and the insane color graphics! It's very unique and can be very challenging with a lot of gameplay. There's also a newer addition where users have uploaded their own boards you can play. There's a lot of content on here to keep you busy for a long time. Amazing game! So much fun!

reviews.review_title: I ♡ PW! BUT, I have a suggestion:
reviews.review_description:
1st: my suggestion:  The scoring/health bar, or w/e you call it, REALLY obstructs my view. PLEASE make it smaller and/or more transparent... like WAYYY more! (who really has time to even glance at it anyways, when you have a ****-ton of  bullets whizzing by your skull?! 😂
REQUEST: More music & maps please!!
I never thought I'd love any other vr game as much as Beat Saber. The music, good beats, bright neon colors.. its an 80's / music lovers DREAM! AND, given the fact that I never would have imagined myself PLAYING "video games", let alone actually ADVANCING (to E+!!)further than I ever thought my 54 yr old brain could ever handle. Well, this isn't colorful per-say its kind of retro-style, but it's so satisfying and fun..and totally addicting!! You can customize all of the different guns to your liking. My favorite gun is the one that shoots/sprays like 4 bullets at a time! You can also set it to where you don't need to reload too.   Pistol whipping the crap out of guys that are like 6.5' tall, shooting to the Beat of the music, dodging bullets... I dance in my living room & yell & curse at the guys when they shoot me.. having a blast! (pretty sure my neighbors hate me lol) Definitely a good work out if you adjust the settings. Shooting, punching, bopping to the music, bob & weave, squatting..ugh... it's so fun!!
NOW... if they'd only update it with MORE!!!
Nuff said
This is probably my favorite VR game. It’s the one I keep coming back to and one of my “showcase” games for people new to VR.

Amazing how quickly you turn into John Wick with a dash of Matrix style bullet dodging , topped off with a wicked soundtrack.

I thought I’d discovered shoot ‘em up heaven, and then I found the 2 gun setting! Oh yes!!!

Great workout and brutally satisfying. Can’t see it getting boring anytime
Soon.

The gameplay is great and a lot of fun. Also the art style is phenomenal!

But the performance is bad. The resolution on my Quest 3 is the worst resolution of all games i own. It's so blurry that the game is nearly unplayable. (On pcvr the game occasionally stutters so it seems a deeper problem with the coding)
Played this for the first time today, haven't had that much fun in a WHILE. Had so much fun I dislocated my kneecap while ducking down! Went back it right after, 8/10 experience.
When I saw the pictures I wasn't sure if it would be good But it's extremely good If you like shooting then this game is for you. Must have
This game helped me learn how to aim dodge bullets and much more it makes me just feel good about myself i highly recomend this game

reviews.review_title: Perfect for gaming on your exercise bike
reviews.review_description:
I use the modifiers for no obstacles and slow bullets so I don't fall off my exercise bike. With these enabled it is an absolute killer app for a workout on the bike. The continuous movement fits so well for this use case.

How mind boggling is the continuous content by Cloudhead Games? No DLC costs. All for one price. Outstanding in these times. That's why I bought it twice: on PC and also on Quest. Chefs kiss.
Reload doesn't work and tutorial Doesn't explain how to dodge or duck. So stupid. Have to figure everything out by trial and error
Love this game

reviews.review_title: Makes you feel like John Wick
reviews.review_description: Love everything about this game. Good music, fun levels, fun options for weapons and making levels harder.
Got this with horizon plus. Doesn’t work.
At least i make some squats with this game. Recomended, its adictive and great game

reviews.review_title: Great spin on the rhythm genre
reviews.review_description: Its uncanny how some user made content is way better than the official one

reviews.review_title: This is why I bought VR
reviews.review_description: Read the title it’s amazing and there is so much content.
Please add more campaigns to this i love your game.

This is the best VR rhythm game and one of my favorite VR games/video games/physical activities of all time.

I have Beat Saber, Synth Riders, Ragnarok, and PW beats them all because you can move and shoot freely as long, just as you don't get hit and you shoot to the beat.

The modifiers make it even better, dodging bullets is just as much fun as shooting in this game so you can ramp up the incoming fire and rack up points that way. Or just set yourself to "melee" and eliminate targets by punching their projectiles back at them.

Make sure you try out the suggested styles and not just the basics - this game is at its best when styles and modifiers come into play and you can find all sorts of combinations that bring out different movement and playstyles from you.

In addition to all the excellent maps from the developers there are also a multitude of user created maps, many of which rival the originals 💪

Benefits to coordination, muscle control, strength, flexibility, reflexes.

Must play 🔫🦵

reviews.review_title: I would buy a headset just for this game
reviews.review_description: This is my favorite game. I just wish they would make a sequel
Not close

reviews.review_title: Amazing game I love to play this outside
reviews.review_description: I saw this game and thought imagine playing this outside so I got it went outside and played it and I loved it

Pistol Whip evolved from just shooting and punching into a game of extreme flexibility, challenge, and creativity, giving players the opportunity to customize guns, use melee weapons, play tennis with bullets, and, overall, play the game how they want to. Players are even given the freedom to play levels that other players had created. Pistol Whip has become an incredible game. It has very little flaws, and has excellent gameplay.
Probably the only major flaw is getting too easy, although that’s not too huge of a flaw. It’s rather a minor flaw more than anything. The base songs get extremely easy the more you continuously play them. This flaw, however, is slowly dying, as remixes provide great challenges for players who are really good at the game. One very good example is the song very famous for its appearance across many other rhythm games, including Rock Band and Guitar Hero games: Through the Fire and Flames. That song has always retained its insane difficulty, so players seeking a challenge could just look for that, and them the game isn’t completely easy anymore.
Overall, Pistol Whip may have had a huge issue with levels getting too easy, however, exploiting that flaw and eliminating it makes Pistol Whip a lot better. Also to mention that the amount of customizations there are also contribute to the excellence of Pistol Whip. Pistol Whip has become an incredible rhythm game that is definitely worth playing.
I love this game because it supports (my k3d gun) this add-on takes the gaming experience to a much higher level! search for it: K3design dot store

reviews.review_title: The best game buy over beat saber
reviews.review_description: It’s never lagged once for me I got it with meta horizon plus but I’m canceling my subscription now I have to buy this game and thirty dollars is worth it 100% i recommend if you want a game like beat saber but with weapons
Really good game
This is my favorite active game in my game library
The game is awesome...I like the looks, the sound, and the action....

Really love this game for exercise! You feel like a true action hero. The enemies are super satisfying and stress-relieving to hit and I love the physical exercise like dodging and ducking in the game. My only two complaints are that it’s not multiplayer (unlike Beat Saber) and the lack of song genres. Overall, I highly recommend this game to anyone who likes satisfying shooting games!
Even for a workout its a great game to get some exercise, dodge some shots and pistol whip some Mfers. So much fun!

After playing the demo of red hot and not thinking a great deal to it I was worried this would be too similar.  

It isn't it's way better imo

Totally the f word bad A@@@e

Oculus quest must own!

6/5

Was Sorry I procrastinated!

reviews.review_title: I love it. My personal favorite
reviews.review_description: I think it’s one of - maybe even <the> best - fun shooter game in the store. It was one of the first games I played and I play it ever since. It’s the perfect game for an hour of fun shooting with friends and doesn’t cause any motion sickness in contrast to other shooter games.

I love the different
 worlds i can play in ...so much fun..im never dizzy..
I kind of meh when I was looking at the game but after I got it I didn’t regret it, nearly perfectly.￼

I am not a big fan of fast edm songs or whatever they're called. Tbh I don't know what I expected of the game, should've seen that coming. However I am a big fan of boom town in the cowboy style campaign. 
Only the first song of that campaign was good tho since the rest were just shotguns which were boring and unsatiafying to use.
As I said, there's only one fun and good song in the game. Still good, gopw they add more of that style and not just ear destroying screech songs
Not the best game but fun to dodge and play

reviews.review_title: One of the best games I've played
reviews.review_description:
Extremely fun like the cowboy one a bit more though maybe they can do stuff with other things maybe Star wars who knows I love it and everybody should 
 get it
i've been playing this game for a few years now, it never gets boring.

I recently got a quest and was struggling to find good games, I redeemed this and gave it a shot and absolutely loved it. I could spend an entire day on this truthfully. Very satisfying, long levels, gets your brain moving.
Richard simmons + John wick +  acid + rave music - my pinky toe = this game

When this game came out, I thought it was pretty good, some good songs and simple yet satisfying gameplay. Since then, with the numerous updates it has received, it has only gone from strength to strength. You can tell that the developers really learnt from the earlier levels as the later ones became more and more dynamic and better at integrating the level's song. Over the years this has become a robust experience and best of all, the new levels have all been completely FREE. This is the type of dev behavior that we ought to be encouraging
Workout game. First mission is hella fun. 2nd mission using gun to melee, instant uninstall.

My fat a** decided to download some workout games but downloaded this one by pure chance and tried it out when i had the time but boy was i mistaken, this was not a chill, beat saber like game,it's a full on workout. Other workout apps will have you doing deep squats that make you legs wobble but i found myself a** to the grass (without really noticing)because i didnt wabt a fictional bullet touching me. Anyways, if you wanna lose weight is the right game because if you're not fast enough and acchrate enough, you're moving but if you get more reactive ( a good skill to have in real life), you are rewarded by it!

This game is SO much fun. You guys should adopt a similar model like walkabout mini golf where you release paid content every 1-2 months. I'd be very happy to pay for it and this way further development is more sustainable! Keep up the amazing work!!
Pair this with beat Sabre and you have an amazing cardio session. The calorie burn fir an hour playtime is pretty decent to!

This was a quest + purchase. Wasn't sure about a rhythm game with guns but it was actually entertaining and fun to play. Moving around obstacles does require a nonstationary play area as obstacles require more than leaning or bending. It's not a bad thing if you got the space. I liked the 2 campaigns stories but they are short. I would really like more of them (would've added a 5th star). Music isn't exactly my taste but it's at least not annoying. I'd prefer more 80s style or rocknroll. Still can't figure what they mean by shooting with the beat (maybe a more visual cue at least for beginners)
Ok biggest gripe so far is in the highscoreing. They need to put how many people are on the board total. There is a big difference being In the top ten between 10 people and 10,000

The gameplay is great, a fun on rails shooter with some dodging bullets and terrain. All of the music is very subdued and mediocre so it doesn't really add to the experience. I find it's best to play your own music over the ingame music. 

The only other issue is if you lose you start over from the beginning which is only made worse with the boring soundtrack.
The only game where I can shoot cowboys to the beat of Folsom Prison Blues by Johnny Cash (mod, there is an in game mod manager).

... where after many many hours I keep coming back. It looks awesome, difficulty levels are flexible so you can have chill and challanging experience depending on your mood, the only thing to bear in mind is that music is on the EDM side, so if you expect many different genres this may not be your cup of tea
I feel so cool until I turn off aim assist and then I realize I was shooting yards off my target. I am sore like leg day after two hours of this. My favorite game so far.
Wow! What a brilliant game, great music, great atmosphere of thr different levels and highly engaging... JW would be proud 💪
